commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the message-delivery semantics change; there is no safe automated repair to queue.; Cleared: The diff reduces a raw provider-error disclosure path and does not add dependency, workflow, credential, or broader code-execution surface. (sha=3a970a33dc2355caf39904d71b480888f27e9c09)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a fatal structured error flag to cron payload resolution, short-circuits isolated announce completion delivery before `dispatchCronDelivery`, and adds a regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `main` classifies the structured `isError` payload as fatal and still passes its `deliveryPayloads` into `dispatchCronDelivery`. I did not run a live Discord or Telegram cron reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the message-delivery semantics change; there is no safe automated repair to queue.

target.title: fix(cron): suppress fatal error completion announce
target.body:
Fixes #42243.

## Summary
- short-circuit normal cron completion delivery only when an isolated run resolves to a fatal structured `isError` payload
- keep controlled fatal failure signals on the existing delivery path, preserving existing cron behavior
- keep the cron run marked as error with diagnostics and delivery trace state, but avoid direct announce fallback with provider error JSON

## Validation
- `git diff --check`
- `node /Users/sergio/Projects/openclaw-contrib/node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A cron isolated-agent run with `delivery.mode=announce` and a fatal structured `isError` payload no longer enters the normal completion announce path, so provider error JSON is not sent to the configured channel as the cron completion message. Controlled synthesized fatal failure signals still go through the existing delivery path.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw PR checkout at `3a970a33dc3a4d783b9d577ed1ff15f1476a4f49` on macOS with Node `v25.6.1`, running the cron isolated-agent delivery policy path against the patched source.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node /Users/sergio/Projects/openclaw-contrib/node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/sergio/Projects/Global Openclaw/tmp/openclaw-cron-42243

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  37 passed (37)
Duration  5.17s
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The new `does not dispatch announce delivery for fatal error payloads` case passed, and the existing `delivers synthesized fatal failure signals even when the original payloads are empty` case also passed. Together they prove raw structured error payloads skip `dispatchCronDelivery`, while controlled synthesized failure messages still dispatch.
- **What was not tested**: A live Discord or Telegram send with real channel credentials was not run locally; this patch exercises the cron delivery decision before external channel transport.
target.author: sercada
